prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: ★★★☆☆  Waking up arrived on schedule and performs its basic function reliably: it transitions the user from unconsciousness to consciousness with predictable variability depending on settings (alarm tone, ambient light, caffeine availability). No assembly required; the interface is simple (open eyes, sit up, think), though initial responsiveness can be sluggish and occasional side effects—headache, confusion, brief regret—may occur without mitigation (coffee or sunlight recommended). The snooze feature is convenient but encourages cumulative delay and diminished satisfaction. Durability is acceptable for daily use, but performance improves with regular maintenance (consistent bedtime) and degrades after prolonged misuse. Overall functional and unavoidable, with room for improvement in user experience.
